title: Prevent Homelessness Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Prevent Homelessness Act of 2025 creates a special fund called the Housing Stabilization Fund to help very poor families stay in their homes. This fund gives money to local groups that help families pay for rent, utilities, and home repairs so they do not become homeless. The government will put one hundred million dollars into this fund every year from 2027 to 2031. The money also pays for support services to help families with their housing needs.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Prevent Homelessness Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Housing Stabilization Fund
sections.text:
The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development must create and manage a special fund called the Housing Stabilization Fund. This fund will give money each year to local groups that help very poor families avoid losing their homes.

To get money from this fund, local groups must run emergency housing help programs that meet certain rules. These programs must be run by or chosen by continuum of care groups (local organizations that coordinate homeless services in their area). The programs must work in the same area as these local groups.

These emergency programs can only help extremely low income families and very low income families (families that make very little money). These families must be unable to pay for housing costs because of money problems. Money problems include losing a job, getting sick, family emergencies, unexpected bills, or living in dangerous or unhealthy conditions. Families must show proof they cannot pay, like past due rent notices, eviction notices, or shut off notices from utility companies.

The programs must work together with local systems that decide which homeless people get help first. They must also meet other rules that the Secretary creates.

Money from grants can be used to help families pay for housing costs like future rent payments, but not for more than eight months in any twelve month period. The monthly help cannot be more than what the Secretary decides is reasonable compared to similar rental homes in the area. The money can also pay past due rent based on the actual amount owed.

For homeowners, the money can pay mortgage payments including past due amounts, but not for more than eight months in any twelve month period. The monthly amount cannot exceed what the Secretary decides is appropriate.

The money can pay utility bills including past due amounts. It can pay for housing repairs needed to make homes safe to live in, like insulation, window repairs, door repairs, and roof repairs.

The grants can pay for services to help with mental health problems, domestic violence, child welfare, job counseling, drug treatment, and other services. They can pay for housing counseling and advice, including outreach, mediation, and case management.

The money can pay for legal help with evictions, foreclosures, and other housing problems. It can pay security deposits and other short term costs that help families keep stable housing, like gas, groceries, car repairs, bus passes, furniture, application fees, and family reunification services.

To get a grant, continuum of care groups must submit applications that the Secretary approves. These applications must have information the Secretary needs to make sure the grant money is used correctly.

In fiscal year two thousand twenty seven, all available money will be given out using a formula the Secretary creates. In later years, eighty percent of the money will be given out using the formula, and twenty percent will be given out through a competition.

The Secretary must create a formula that considers how many extremely low income families live in each area, including homeless people living outside who spend too much of their income on housing. The formula must also consider how many very low income families live in each area. The Secretary will use data from the most recent Point in Time Homeless Count to make these decisions.

The Secretary must create a competition for twenty percent of the funding. This competition will be based on measurable standards that do not create new paperwork burdens for applicants. The standards will favor programs that help the poorest households, use other non federal money, prevent homelessness before it happens, and use problem solving methods to help families stay housed while connecting them to other government benefits.

Important definitions include continuum of care groups, which are organizations that work together in a geographic area to help homeless people. Extremely low income family and very low income family have the meanings given in the United States Housing Act of nineteen thirty seven. Fund means the Housing Stabilization Fund. Secretary means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

Congress authorizes one hundred million dollars to be appropriated for this fund for each fiscal year from two thousand twenty seven through two thousand thirty one.
